title: Multi-Account Platform Vision
author: amit
summary: Evolve Decifer from a single-account trading bot into a multi-account platform that can run independent strategies across separate IBKR accounts with unified monitoring and risk management.

sections.heading: Why Multi-Account
sections.content: Running multiple strategies on a single account creates position conflicts, makes P&L attribution difficult, and limits risk isolation. Separate accounts per strategy (or strategy group) solve all three problems while enabling cleaner performance measurement.

sections.heading: Architecture Changes
sections.content: The core change is making account_id a first-class parameter throughout the stack — orders, positions, risk, and dashboard all become account-aware. The IBKR API already supports multiple accounts via Financial Advisor (FA) or separate logins.

sections.heading: Phased Rollout
sections.content: Phase E in the roadmap covers six features (feat-019 through feat-024) that build multi-account support incrementally: aggregation first, then per-account risk, order routing, correlation guards, unified journaling, and finally comparative analytics.

sections.heading: Risk Model
sections.content: Each account gets its own risk envelope (max drawdown, position limits, sector caps). A cross-account correlation guard prevents the combined portfolio from taking on unintended concentrated exposure. Global kill-switch remains — one button stops all accounts.

sections.heading: Success Criteria
sections.content: The platform is ready when: (1) two strategies can run on separate accounts simultaneously without interference, (2) the dashboard shows per-account and combined metrics, (3) risk limits are enforced independently per account, and (4) the trade journal correctly attributes every trade.

open_questions: FA account vs separate logins — which is simpler for paper?
Should strategies be allowed to share an account if desired?
How to handle margin calculation across accounts?
